accepted.reason: Dense linear-algebra style forward-pass loops are high-value optimization targets for Cython conversion.
Magic-formula tire-force computations are numeric kernels with repeated transcendental operations and clear optimization utility.
Nested-loop beam-splitter transforms provide substantial arithmetic workload ideal for performance-focused training examples.
Block downsample/upsample array kernels are canonical stencil-style loops suited to Cython speedup tasks.
Function-train core evaluation uses structured nested loops over basis coefficients and maps well to optimization workflows.

skipped.reason: Async pipe/event-loop integration code is I/O and runtime orchestration, not a standalone numeric optimization problem.
Cython compiler/runtime test fixture for memoryview behavior is not an optimization-training workload.
Tiny settings/debug flag module lacks meaningful computational content for Cython speedup training.
Tutorial/demo code is primarily pedagogical and not a realistic optimization target.
Memoryview pickling/unpickling utility is type-conversion glue with limited value for optimization-centric training.
